steps.title: Right-leaning AND chain — depth 4
steps.narrative: Source: chain.blif with f = ((((a·b)·c)·d)·e). After strash this is 4 right-leaning ANDs with depth 4. balance (Dar_ManBalance) is purely associativity-based: it leaves the function unchanged but reorders operands inside maximal AND-trees so they implement at minimum depth.

panes.title: Why depth matters
panes.body: Depth (level) is the longest fanin-2 AIG path from any PI to a PO; it bounds critical-path delay after mapping. balance does not change AND count — it just shapes the tree so subsequent rewrite/refactor moves see regular structure.

steps.title: Step 1 — collect supergate (Dar_BalanceCone_rec)
steps.narrative:
Walk down through children that are same-type (AND), non-shared (Aig_ObjRefs == 1) ANDs. Stop and record a leaf at every PI or fanout-cone boundary. For the chain, each internal AND is fanout-1 so the recursion bottoms out at all 5 PIs.

Result: vSuper = [e, d, c, b, a] (descending Id order at the moment of insertion).

panes.body: Dar_BalanceUniqify next sorts the leaves by literal and folds x·x = x, x·¬x = 0. All 5 here are distinct PIs, so no change.

steps.title: Step 2 — Huffman pairing: pop b, a → m1 = a·b
steps.narrative:
Dar_BalanceBuildSuper sorts leaves ascending by level, then pops the two cheapest, ANDs them, and reinserts. All five leaves are at level 0 — ties break by Id, smaller first. b and a pop first.

Each Aig_Oper call goes through structural hashing (Aig_TableLookupTwo): an existing AND is reused if found.

steps.title: Step 3 — pop d, c → m2 = c·d
steps.narrative: Next two cheapest: c and d (both level 0). Pair into m2. The queue now holds two level-1 nodes (m1, m2) and one level-0 leaf (e).

steps.title: Step 4 — pop m2, m1 → m3 = m1·m2
steps.narrative:
The queue now holds [e, m1, m2] with e at level 0 and m1/m2 at level 1. Pure Huffman would pair the two minimum (e, m1), giving an unbalanced result. Dar_BalanceFindLeft instead prefers same-level partners when available — pairing both level-1 nodes first lets the level-0 leaf join nearer the root, keeping the eventual depth balanced.

Result: m3 = m1·m2 at level 2.

steps.title: Step 5 — pop m3, e → m4 = m3·e (the new f)
steps.narrative: Last pair: m3 (level 2) and e (level 0). The output AND is m4 = m3·e at level 3. The supergate's root is now m4; Dar_Balance_rec returns it as the replacement for the original f.

steps.title: Done — same 4 ANDs, depth 4 → 3
steps.narrative:
The original chain and the balanced tree compute the same function (x·x = x, AND is associative and commutative). Node count is unchanged at 4 ANDs because every pair is genuinely new — no strash hit. Depth shrinks 4 → 3 because the AND-tree is now log2-shaped.

This is the cheap depth-only cleanup that runs between every other operator in compress*.

panes.body: Dar_BalancePermute looks one ply ahead: among leaves at the same minimum level, if a (p1, p2) pair already exists in the strash table it biases pairing to trigger reuse. The chain doesn't benefit (no pre-existing pairs), but in larger benchmarks this is where balance also reduces node count.
